findings.title: DICOM C-FIND: patient record query in cleartext
findings.description: A DICOM C-FIND query was detected. This allows querying patient studies, names, IDs, dates, and diagnoses from a PACS/worklist server. PHI is directly exposed in query responses.
findings.recommendation: Immediately restrict C-FIND access to authorized clinical systems only. Enable TLS. Log and alert on all C-FIND queries.

findings.title: Unencrypted HL7 MLLP traffic detected
findings.description: HL7 v2 messages detected over MLLP (port 2575) without TLS. 3 message(s) captured. MLLP has no built-in security — patient records are fully visible on the network.
findings.recommendation: Wrap all MLLP connections in TLS (port 2576). Implement IP allowlisting between HL7 endpoints. Use an integration engine with audit logging.

findings.title: HL7 ADT message exposes patient data
findings.description: An HL7 ADT (Patient admission/discharge/transfer) message was captured in cleartext. This message type directly contains patient PHI.
findings.recommendation: Encrypt all HL7 transport. Review data minimization — send only required fields.

findings.title: FHIR Bearer token transmitted in cleartext
findings.description: An Authorization: Bearer token was observed in an unencrypted HTTP request to a FHIR endpoint. This token can be replayed to access patient data.
findings.recommendation: Enforce HTTPS immediately. Rotate all exposed tokens. Implement token binding where possible.

findings.title: Unencrypted DICOM traffic detected
findings.description: DICOM traffic observed on port 104 without TLS. Medical images and patient metadata may be transmitted in cleartext.
findings.recommendation: Enable DICOM TLS (port 2762) as per DICOM PS 3.15 Annex B. Restrict DICOM ports to known AE titles only.

findings.title: DICOM C-STORE: medical image transfer in cleartext
findings.description: A DICOM C-STORE command was detected, indicating medical images are being transferred. Images contain embedded patient metadata (DICOM tags: PatientName, PatientID, DOB, StudyDescription).
findings.recommendation: Enforce TLS for all DICOM C-STORE operations. Audit which systems are storing images and where.

findings.title: FHIR Patient resource accessed over HTTP
findings.description: Unencrypted HTTP request to FHIR endpoint detected. Resource type: Patient. FHIR over HTTP exposes patient data and auth tokens in cleartext.
findings.recommendation: All FHIR endpoints must use HTTPS/TLS 1.2+. Disable HTTP listener on FHIR server. Enforce SMART on FHIR authorization.

findings.title: DICOM Association without authentication
findings.description: DICOM Association Request detected. Standard DICOM has no built-in authentication — any node can query the SCP. Patient data may be retrievable via C-FIND/C-GET.
findings.recommendation: Implement AE Title whitelisting on the SCP. Deploy DICOM TLS mutual authentication.

findings.title: SNMP-managed Medical Device detected
findings.description: SNMP traffic observed from a medical device. SNMPv1/v2 uses community strings transmitted in cleartext.
findings.recommendation: Upgrade to SNMPv3 with authentication and encryption. Change default community strings.
